dialog.stepLabel: ??: "Are you trapped here by the rain?..."

items.text: Are you trapped here by the rain?

options.text: No, I'm waiting for someone.
Thank you.

items.text: I happen to be waiting for someone, too.
No need for thanks. We're about to embark on a journey together, {PlayerName}.
I am Changli, your "Wayfinder."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Don't worry. The rain won't last much longer. Our destination lies just beyond it.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName}, what do you know about Mt. Firmament?

options.text: Not much.
It's the place where Jinzhou was founded?
What on earth is going on in that place?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: There is... a certain folk tale. Have you heard of it?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: While hiking in the mountains, a woodcutter came upon two people playing Weiqi and became enthralled with their game.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Upon his return home, he found a world transformed. Everything was different, as if in a new reality.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That mountain was a secret realm where time worked differently. The woodcutter only spent half a day inside, but outside, a century had passed.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Mt. Firmament is such a place.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But unlike this folk tale, there is a price to pay for entering and exiting its grounds.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If I spend a full day in the mountains, ten days will be taken off my lifespan upon my leaving, reflecting the actual time that has passed outside.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Not to mention... the situation on Mt. Firmament right now is probably even more complex than before.

options.text: What happened?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The strange time patterns of Mt. Firmament are tied to the Sentinel's Temporal Mandate. Time ebbs and flows at its will.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If the Fractsidus really has imprisoned our Sentinel, causing it to lose control of its powers, that could explain the Temporal Disruptions... which means Mt. Firmament is basically a dead end with no way out.

options.text: And you are heading straight into that dead end.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Lifespan is not the top of my concerns. If you've decided to venture there, I'll join you as your Wayfinder.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Jinhsi has stayed on Mt. Firmament for too long.

options.text: Sanhua said she might be in danger.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: She doesn't miss a thing, does she?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Before Jinhsi left for Mt. Firmament, scales began appearing on her body, and her Resonance Ability was weakening.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Those signs pointed to a dire situation for the Sentinel as well.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That's why I must lead you there. You might have the key to solving our dilemma.

options.text: What do you mean?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: You're here because I invited you. Sanhua was instructed to disclose my whereabouts if you ever went to City Hall and asked about Jinhsi.

options.text: Why would you tell me?
How did you know I'd go to the City Hall?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: From what I understand, there is a strong link between you and the Sentinel. More importantly, you've been the primary target of the Fractsidus.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's no surprise that you went to City Hall for answers, considering your memory loss and Jinhsi's prolonged absence.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I need to tell you the truth... Because you've been the top priority—No, the sole concern of our enemy. The Fractsidus.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That Overseer tried everything to sway you and make you join their side before our battle with the reviving Threnodian.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's a clear sign that your involvement could disrupt their plans, especially at Mt. Firmament.

options.text: Why do they care so much about me?
It seems everyone's interested in me...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Records at the Grand Library tell a tale of someone who dared to enter Mt. Firmament after the Sentinel's descent. And they emerged unscathed, as if time had no hold on them.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: They were also said to have "stood alongside the Sentinel."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And here before me stands someone who bears an uncanny resemblance to that very individual.

options.text: You think that person is me?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Is it you? If not you, who else could it be?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It took me some effort to finally reach this conclusion.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName}, the journey ahead is filled with danger at every turn. Once we venture into the mountains, there's no turning back. Are you sure you still want to join me?

options.text: Yes. The strange time pattern can't hurt me.
I am the key to solving your dilemma. Then why ask?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: As your Wayfinder, I'll lead you to your destination. What you do after that is entirely up to you.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The rain is subsiding, and the sky will soon clear.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName}, it's time for us to head out.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So it can't hurt me...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Time on Mt. Firmament used to simply flow slower than in the outside world. Visitors were safe as long as they remained within the Temporal Barrier.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But what we're seeing now is different. The flow of time has gone haywire. This is what we call the Temporal Disruptions...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: What was once slow and steady has become chaotic and turbulent. Some areas are frozen in time, while others speed up or slow down unpredictably.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: No one knows how much they'll age after leaving Mt. Firmament, but aging from youth to old age may be a blessing compared to the unknown fate that awaits.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Leaving Mt. Firmament could turn any mortal to dust in just a moment. Such is the power of time within its walls.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: These poor creatures, trapped in the Temporal Disruption, are the most pitiful of all... Their bodies and minds have been ravaged by the chaotic time, leaving them in unimaginable suffering...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: So, it looks like the Fractsidus did manage to capture our Sentinel, leading to the Temporal Disruptions we are facing now.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I had wished that my theories would turn out wrong...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...As expected, my Terminal isn't working. We can't reach Jinhsi.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The Temporal Disruption is preventing it from functioning.

options.text: Jinhsi...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We can't waste a moment. Jinhsi's fate isn't the only one at stake—all the inhabitants of Mt. Firmament are in danger.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We need to hurry to Hongzhen, a town nestled at the heart of Mt. Firmament. Jinhsi would likely pass through there on her search for the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: What's the matter, {PlayerName}?

options.text: I dreamed of this place...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: You dreamed of Hongzhen?
You tell Changli about the flashing images you saw in your dream.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It sounds like you did dream of this place.

options.text: I dreamed of Hongzhen being attacked by TDs.
I dreamed of the Sentinel protecting people here.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: What you just described closely aligns with the accounts documented in our Grand Library.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Over a century ago, a vast horde of Tacet Discords invaded Huanglong. Hongzhen suffered greatly during that onslaught.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Thankfully, the Sentinel descended, manipulating time to erect a protective barrier, keeping those monsters at bay.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName}, perhaps... That dream is more than just a dream.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It could be a glimpse into your own past, and... a guiding light for the future.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Hongzhen is a pivotal location, linking various parts of Mt. Firmament. With the Fractsidus now targeting us, they'll seize any opportunity to intercept our path.

options.text: They've laid a trap in Hongzhen.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But every trap has its weak points.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Someone has used their Resonance Ability here, but it feels different from the Fractsidus' aggression... It's Jinhsi.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And it seems like she used it in a dire situation. Could it be related to the people of Hongzhen?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The traces lead deeper into the heart of Hongzhen.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Let's keep moving, {PlayerName}. Don't let your guard down.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The bridge has broken...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Judging by its design and intricate details, it must be a creation of the Court of Savantae.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: This is what they call a Chronosorter.

options.text: What is that?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It mimics the Sentinel's <ano=Ability to control time>Temporal Mandate</ano>, able to reverse small-scale events and restore objects to their previous state.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: My master once told me about the Court of Savantae and their fearless quest to unravel the mysteries of Mt. Firmament, risking all in their pursuit of understanding the strange flow of time.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Their dedication bore fruit in the form of fascinating inventions, such as this Chronosorter.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But it doesn't add up...

options.text: What's wrong?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Why hasn't this brilliant invention spread beyond Mt. Firmament?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: This Chronosorter was built beyond the Temporal Barrier, so its creator must have already paid the price for leaving.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But even if they died, there are countless other <ano=Court of Savantae Committee>CSC members</ano> who could replicate it. How did such advanced technology become lost?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Or... do these Chronosorters stop functioning once they are removed from Mt. Firmament?

options.text: What should we do now?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: This Chronosorter, weathered though it may appear, bears no signs of neglect. Someone has tended to it diligently.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Let's give it a try.

dialog.stepLabel: Xinyi: "I believe you are here for Magistrate Jinhsi?..."

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I believe you are here for Magistrate Jinhsi?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: The Magistrate left Hongzhen about fifteen minutes ago. She should be at the Truthseeker's Pass now.

options.text: The Truthseeker's Pass?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: That's what they call it in Hongzhen. You might know it better as the ruins of a Court of Savantae facility.

options.text: What is Jinhsi doing there?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Lady Xinyi, please elaborate.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Madam Magistrate, are you...

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: I'm… fine…

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: How's the situation? Have we evacuated all the civilians?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Yes. Remaining civilians have been counted and escorted to safety.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Good. These hidden chambers should provide some much-needed shelter for them to rest a little...

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Despite being the Sentinel's Appointed Resonator, I haven’t been able to sense its presence for some time now…

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: I can feel my Resonance Ability waning, and I get my power from the Sentinel. This can only mean one thing: the Sentinel's condition is getting worse.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Lady Xinyi, I need to know where the Sentinel was last seen.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: I can only fix the Temporal Disruption within a confined radius. It's already pushing my limits to keep this small pocket of space secure.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Mt. Firmament is in serious trouble, and we can't waste any more time. We need our Sentinel to stabilize the time flow.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: The Sentinel was last seen flying towards Mianloong Chamber, where it would normally rest. The Fractsidus likely swarmed it soon after.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: But little did they know, we had a defense system in place, built with the help of the Court of Savantae. It was reactivated as soon as our Sentinel made it back inside.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: This mechanism has allowed us to locate their troops, and we've already eliminated some of them outside the Mianloong Chamber.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: To get inside Mianloong Chamber, we have to find the Cipher Key in Truthseeker's Pass and unseal it with the Keyforge.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Understood. I will go bring back the Cipher Key.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: You are injured. Please rest and leave it to me.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: The Fractsidus will most likely attack this place once I leave. You must stay here and guard it.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: I implore you, Xinyi. Help me keep our people safe.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: ...Yes, I will, Madam Magistrate.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Before I headed to Mt. Firmament, the Fractsidus revealed a prophecy from our Sentinel: a fateful clash between the Sentinel and myself that would shape the fate of our city.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: I never intended to believe their baseless claims, but I was not surprised to hear it.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: The Sentinel had told me long ago that there would come a day when I surpass it in power. A day when the humans of Jinzhou take control of their own destiny.

items.speaker: Jinhsi
items.text: Once we save the Sentinel from danger, I intend to have a serious conversation with it about this prophecy.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: ...And then she left.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: There's still one thing that doesn't add up—How did the Fractsidus kidnap our Sentinel?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The Mianloong Chamber was once renovated by the Court of Savantae. Do you think their research could be involved somehow?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I'll look into what you said, but once we have the Cipher Key, the Mianloong Chamber will be left vulnerable to the Fractsidus' potential retaliation.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We must keep them at bay...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName} and I are headed to the Truthseeker's Pass to meet up with Jinhsi. We'll distract the Fractsidus main forces, giving you an opportunity to stage your ambush.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: And the locals will handle the rest.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I will send some men to open up the side exit for you. Head south and keep walking a couple of minutes, and you will see the Truthseeker's Pass downstream.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Thank you, Lady Xinyi. Please leave it to us.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: In this place she was born, and in this place she returns. Our Magistrate's fate is bound to Mt. Firmament...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Someone fought here against those Fractsidus...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Must be Jinhsi. Her trails led to this location.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Using her Resonance Ability too much would only worsen her condition...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But she must have had a reason. Was she helping the people of Hongzhen find refuge?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The entrance is sealed.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's the Photon Barrier. Another invention from the Court of Savantae.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Specific steps must be followed to undo the barrier. {PlayerName}, I'll guide you.

dialog.stepLabel: Changli: "Use long-range weapons on the Photonvaults to cut off its en..."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Use long-range weapons on the Photonvaults to cut off its energy.
